lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Toons Universe Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Use this module as your quick code desk: copy the active code, redeem it before queueing, and track where new drops are posted.

steps.title: Redeem the active code first
steps.description: Current code: THANKYOU. Redeem it for 500 Malachor before your next run to secure early currency.

steps.title: Use the left-side redeem button
steps.description: Open the game in Roblox, tap Redeem Code on the left panel, then paste the code exactly.

steps.title: Verify rewards immediately
steps.description: After tapping Redeem, confirm your Malachor balance changed before entering the elevator queue.

steps.title: Track code channels weekly
steps.description: Monitor the Roblox page, Discord announcements, Roblox group updates, and major code trackers.

quickTips: Paste codes directly to avoid typo failures.
Redeem before shopping so you can compare unlock options immediately.
Keep expired codes in a separate list to reduce confusion for new visitors.
Update this module whenever official channels post fresh reward drops.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Toons Universe Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The early loop is straightforward: choose a stable agent, complete machines safely, manage stamina, then reach the elevator before panic pressure ends the floor.

cards.name: Choose a safe starter
cards.description: Nimbus is a forgiving solo pick. Weffle is an easy support option for duo and group runs.

cards.name: Prioritize machines over loot
cards.description: Objective completion controls pace. Loot only when nearby routes are clear.

cards.name: Save stamina for danger windows
cards.description: Do not sprint constantly. Keep stamina reserve for line-of-sight breaks and elevator pushes.

cards.name: Coordinate before panic mode
cards.description: When panic starts, stop side tasks and call a safe route back to the elevator together.

milestones: Reach elevator during panic mode with team intact

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Toons Universe Tier List
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: This practical tier snapshot prioritizes consistency for public lobbies and early progression rather than niche high-risk setups.

items.name: S Tier: Nimbus
items.description: Free access, strong stamina sustain, and reliable recovery windows for long chases.

items.name: S Tier: Weffle
items.description: Strong speed utility for teammates and steady value in coordinated objective runs.

items.name: A Tier: Patcher / Phantasia / Copper / Selena
items.description: Excellent support and information tools with strong team payoff when coordinated.

items.name: B Tier: Discard / Koi / Squiddle / Teipo
items.description: Useful kits that perform best when positioning and route discipline are already stable.

items.name: C Tier: Guido and Rossum
items.description: Objective-focused value but demands careful pathing because movement and stamina are limited.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Toons Universe Agents List
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Use this compressed roster to decide who to unlock next based on role fit, team need, and consistency under pressure.

solutions.name: Nimbus
solutions.description: Free starter with strong stamina recovery, great for players learning chase pacing.

solutions.name: Weffle
solutions.description: Good duo/group support with movement utility that helps teammates stabilize runs.

solutions.name: Fizzle / Selena
solutions.description: Useful for tracking threats or redirecting pressure while objectives are being handled.

solutions.name: Patcher / Copper / Phantasia
solutions.description: Healing, shielding, and buff tools that increase team survival in high-pressure floors.

solutions.name: Discard / Koi / Squiddle / Teipo / Guido and Rossum
solutions.description: Role picks for faster machines, extraction boosts, and structured team comps.

managementTips: Prioritize one dependable pick before broad unlock spending.
Choose unlocks based on your run role, not only novelty.
Pair low-survivability picks with support teammates.
Re-evaluate priorities when major balance updates land.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Toons Universe Agent Unlock Requirements
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Agents are the playable characters in Toons Universe. Use this module to compare Malachor costs, unlock tasks, and role value before buying from Chekhov's Store.

cards.name: Weffle and Nimbus (Free Starters)
cards.description: Both are available when you first join. Pick Weffle for team speed support or Nimbus for solo stamina recovery.

cards.name: Teipo (700) and Discard (1,125)
cards.description: Teipo needs 20 machine extracts, 20 computer hacks, 3 gas leaks, and 25 survived floors. Discard adds larger objective requirements but gives strong objective-speed utility.

cards.name: Squiddle (1,550) and Patcher (1,975)
cards.description: Squiddle is a fast runner that needs fuse and extract progress. Patcher requires ability use and infected encounters, then unlocks reliable team healing.

cards.name: Guido and Rossum (2,400) and Fizzle (2,825)
cards.description: Guido and Rossum reward consistent objective completion. Fizzle requires heavy ability usage and long-floor survival for distraction control.

cards.name: Copper (3,250) and Phantasia (3,675)
cards.description: Copper mixes shielding with blackout utility after deep survival tasks. Phantasia adds speed support and prediction after high ability and gas-leak milestones.

cards.name: Selena (4,100) and Koi (4,500)
cards.description: Selena focuses on information scouting and hacking routes. Koi is a high-cost extraction specialist with emergency invincibility and cooldown reset utility.

cards.name: Solar (Upcoming Agent)
cards.description: Listed as an upcoming Chekhov's Store unlock. Cost and requirements are still marked unknown in current references.

cards.name: Practical Unlock Sequence
cards.description: Use free starters first, buy one low-cost support unlock, then scale into high-cost specialists once your run tasks and Malachor income are stable.

highlights: Track Malachor cost and requirement chains together
Complete task milestones while farming currency
Prioritize one support and one objective specialist early
Use code rewards to accelerate first paid unlocks

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Toons Universe Stats, Sanity, Stamina, and Abilities Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Toons Universe uses bar-based stats. Every agent trades strengths and weaknesses, so role selection should be based on stat spread, not appearance.

regions.name: Health
regions.description: Health controls how many hits you can survive. Higher hearts are safer for new players and distraction roles.

regions.name: Speed
regions.description: Speed decides how quickly you reposition, scout, and exit danger lanes when line-of-sight breaks are available.

regions.name: Stamina
regions.description: Stamina determines how long you can run before recovery, which is critical during extended panic chases.

regions.name: Sanity
regions.description: Sanity affects chase pressure handling. Low-sanity agents need cleaner positioning and earlier disengage timing.

regions.name: Extraction
regions.description: Extraction controls how fast you fill Machines. High-extraction agents speed up floor completion and elevator timing.

regions.name: Hacking
regions.description: Hacking controls computer objective speed. High-hacking picks reduce time spent exposed in objective rooms.

regions.name: Passive Ability
regions.description: Passive abilities trigger automatically under conditions and provide steady value without manual activation.

regions.name: Active Ability
regions.description: Active abilities require timing and cooldown awareness. Good usage can save teammates, reveal threats, or secure escapes.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Toons Universe Malachor Farming and Spending Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Malachor is the main currency for agents and skins. The fastest progress combines code redeems, safe floor clears, objective rewards, capsules, card votes, and efficient spending order.

creatures.name: Redeem Active Codes First
creatures.description: Use active code rewards before queueing. The current 500 Malachor boost can cover most of your first paid unlock.

creatures.name: Complete Floors Consistently
creatures.description: Finish objectives and exit alive for repeatable payouts while also advancing survival requirements for unlock tasks.

creatures.name: Prioritize Objective Work
creatures.description: Machines and computers generate direct rewards. Assign high-extraction or high-hacking roles to keep the loop efficient.

creatures.name: Collect Research Capsules
creatures.description: Pick capsules while rotating between tasks. Safe routing turns small capsule rewards into meaningful long-run gains.

creatures.name: Vote Economy Cards
creatures.description: Pick Job Well Done and Motivate when possible to increase Malachor gained from objectives and capsules.

creatures.name: Stack Unlock Tasks While Farming
creatures.description: Farm floors, hacks, extracts, abilities, fuses, blackouts, and gas leaks together so no run progress is wasted.

creatures.name: Level Agents for Milestones
creatures.description: Regular play grants level-based Malachor milestone rewards, adding extra currency on top of normal floor payouts.

creatures.name: Spend in Clean Unlock Order
creatures.description: Use free agents first, then buy low-cost utility options before committing to expensive specialists.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Toons Universe Infected Guide and Counterplay
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Infecteds are the primary floor threat. Most chases begin from line of sight, while special Infecteds add camera rules, ceiling ambushes, and objective pressure patterns.

items.name: Infected Selena
items.description: She rushes when no one is watching. Assign one player to keep visual contact while the team completes nearby objectives.

items.name: Infected Guido and Rossum
items.description: They strike from ceiling holes and remove health on contact. Check upward routes and avoid passing under active holes.

items.name: Infected Weffle
items.description: Break line of sight with corners, hold stamina for safe exits, and avoid dragging the chase into teammates on objectives.

items.name: Infected Discard
items.description: This roaming threat punishes rushed skill checks and unsafe objective rooms. Keep an escape route and rotate early.

items.name: Infected Koi
items.description: Treat as a standard chase but keep spacing clean so one kiting route does not collapse machine progress.

items.name: Infected Patcher
items.description: Use the same safe fundamentals: spot early, route wide, preserve stamina, and move chases away from objective workers.

unlockMilestones: Maintain line-of-sight discipline and early callouts
Assign a watcher when special infected rules appear
Kite away from objectives and preserve team spacing

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Toons Universe Machines and Skill Checks
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Machines are the core objective on every floor. Clear them cleanly, then rotate back to the Elevator before Panic Mode punishes slow teams.

steps.title: Find the active objectives
steps.details: Each floor spawns Machines, Infecteds, items, capsules, and objective placements. Machine and enemy pressure scale upward as your run goes deeper.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not wander without callouts. Slow searching creates avoidable late-floor pressure.
steps.bestUse: Spread only enough to reveal Machines, then keep return paths visible for quick regrouping.

steps.title: Interact with the Machine
steps.details: Stand by a Machine and interact to begin filling it with Ichor. Objective uptime determines how fast your team reaches safe extraction windows.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not start objective work when an Infected is already pathing toward your room.
steps.bestUse: Give exposed Machines to teammates with safer disengage tools or stronger chase control.

steps.title: Handle Skill Checks carefully
steps.details: Well-timed checks speed progress. Failed checks alert Infecteds and stop momentum at the worst possible time.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not spam inputs under pressure. One failed check can collapse an otherwise clean floor.
steps.bestUse: Slow down and prioritize accuracy whenever team chase pressure is already high.

steps.title: Read the two Machine styles
steps.details: Standard Machines and Computer-style objectives have different interaction rhythms, so your timing habits should adjust per objective type.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not assume every objective UI behaves the same way when checks appear.
steps.bestUse: Assign objective lanes by comfort: extraction-focused players on Machines, hacking-focused players on Computers.

steps.title: Finish as a team before Panic Mode
steps.details: Once all Machines are filled, Panic Mode begins and Infecteds gain speed. The return phase now matters more than extra looting.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not complete the final Machine while teammates are distant, low stamina, or mid-chase.
steps.bestUse: Before finishing the last objective, rotate team positions closer to the Elevator route.

steps.title: Respect the Elevator timer
steps.details: Players who fail to reach the Elevator during Panic Mode are eliminated, even if they still have Hearts.
steps.mistakeToAvoid: Do not chase side pickups after the last objective when return routes are contested.
steps.bestUse: Bank sprint stamina for final extraction instead of spending it during safer machine windows.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Toons Universe Floors and Elevator Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The Elevator connects each floor loop: loadout, generated floor, objective clear, Panic Mode return, then intermission choices that shape the next phase.

cards.title: Loadout Start
cards.summary: Every run opens with a brief setup window before teams enter the Elevator.
cards.whatToDo: Assign roles early: objective worker, scout, protector, or chase stabilizer.
cards.whyItMatters: Clear role intent prevents first-floor confusion and wasted tempo.

cards.title: Generated Floor
cards.summary: Floors spawn objectives, enemy pressure, items, and capsules in shifting layouts.
cards.whatToDo: Locate Machines first, then mark safe returns toward Elevator-side lanes.
cards.whyItMatters: Layout control decides whether split routing stays safe or fails.

cards.title: Machine Scaling
cards.summary: Machine count and threat pressure rise as more floors are cleared.
cards.whatToDo: Expect deeper floors to demand cleaner rotations and fewer check mistakes.
cards.whyItMatters: Late-floor punishment for bad splits or failed checks is significantly higher.

cards.title: Items and Capsules
cards.summary: In-round pickups support both immediate survival and long-term progression.
cards.whatToDo: Collect resources only on safe paths that do not delay extraction timing.
cards.whyItMatters: Greedy detours can erase good objective progress near floor end.

cards.title: Panic Mode Return
cards.summary: After all Machines are filled, extraction urgency sharply increases.
cards.whatToDo: Stop side tasks and commit to team-wide Elevator return routing.
cards.whyItMatters: Missing the return window eliminates players regardless of spare Hearts.

cards.title: Even-Floor Shop
cards.summary: Even floors provide a shop phase with random item options.
cards.whatToDo: Prioritize sustain, safety, and stamina tools before luxury picks.
cards.whyItMatters: Correct purchases prevent snowball failures on the next floor.

cards.title: Odd-Floor Card Vote
cards.summary: Odd floors offer three card choices and majority voting decides the effect.
cards.whatToDo: Vote based on current weakness: safety, stamina, objective speed, or economy.
cards.whyItMatters: Smart votes improve the next floor more than random rarity chasing.

cards.title: Reward Scaling
cards.summary: Surviving deeper floors increases Malachor and progression momentum.
cards.whatToDo: Optimize for repeated clean clears over one risky high-greed push.
cards.whyItMatters: Stable clears outpace wipe-prone runs for long-term unlock speed.

cards.title: Known Floor Styles
cards.summary: Recurring floor patterns reward route memory and objective prediction.
cards.whatToDo: Study repeated objective lanes and Switchbox paths across known layouts.
cards.whyItMatters: Map familiarity reduces panic errors and improves event recovery speed.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Toons Universe Floor Events Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Floor events can alter visibility, stamina economy, and extraction safety. Treat each event as a specific response protocol, not random chaos.

events.whenItHappens: Starts when the Elevator opens and persists through the full floor.
events.warningSigns: Blue haze and noticeably harsher stamina economy from early movement.
events.danger: Sprint reserves vanish faster, making long routes and chase recovery harder.
events.bestReaction: Sprint only for forced escapes, walk safe lanes, and preserve stamina for extraction.
helpfulAgents: Nimbus: emergency stamina refill and low-stamina speed stabilization.
Copper: Guard can save low-stamina teammates during pressure spikes.
helpfulCards: Recover to restore Hearts before compounding mistakes.

events.whenItHappens: Triggers mid-floor and immediately reduces map visibility.
events.warningSigns: Sudden darkness with only local character lighting remaining.
events.danger: Teams lose route awareness, miss objectives, and walk into avoidable enemy lines.
events.bestReaction: Find the Switchbox fast, restore all switches upward, and regroup before over-splitting.
helpfulAgents: Copper: Illuminate improves readability during low-visibility phases.
Copper: Guard protects one teammate in dangerous dark-route transitions.
helpfulCards: Safety to reduce high-class Infected pressure.
Precision to accelerate early objective discovery.

events.whenItHappens: Appears as an Elevator-related floor event during run progression.
events.warningSigns: Event alerts indicate extraction flow will be less forgiving than normal.
events.danger: Scattered teams can be trapped by extended or awkward return timing.
events.bestReaction: Group earlier, save stamina, and finish final objectives only when return routes are clear.
helpfulAgents: Nimbus: stamina sustain and emergency speed for long returns.
Copper: Guard support during final route rotations.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Toons Universe Cards and Voting Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Card votes happen after odd floors. Choose effects that solve your current run problem, not the flashiest option on screen.

cards.bestPickWhen: Your team is already stable and wants stronger objective economy.
You are farming unlock currency without immediate survival pressure.
Your routes are safely collecting capsules on most floors.

cards.effect: Reveals all objectives briefly at round start.
cards.bestPickWhen: Teams are losing time searching Machines on unfamiliar layouts.
Objective pace is the bottleneck and survival control is stable.
Computer objectives are common and your team has hacking specialists.
Your comp depends on active abilities for rescue or safety control.

cards.effect: Adds 5 seconds to Elevator retrieval time.
cards.bestPickWhen: Teammates repeatedly arrive late to extraction windows.
Gas Leak or repeated chase pressure is draining your team.
Long return paths require deeper sprint reserves.
Current floor control is stable and you can invest into future value.

cards.effect: Reduces chance of floor events appearing.
cards.bestPickWhen: Event pressure is creating frequent route collapses.

cards.effect: Reduces chance of higher-class Infecteds appearing.
cards.bestPickWhen: The team is losing too many fights against high-threat spawns.

cards.effect: Heals each damaged Agent by 1 Heart.
cards.bestPickWhen: Multiple teammates are injured before a risky next floor.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Toons Universe Items and Shops Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Use items to heal, speed up objectives, survive chases, and close risky floors. Buy around your next failure point, not habit spending.

items.name: Coffee
items.buyAdvice: Buy when a runner must kite an Infected or force a safe elevator return.

items.name: Mint
items.buyAdvice: Buy when machines are stalling the floor and one player can anchor fast hacks.

items.name: Gloves
items.buyAdvice: Excellent value when machine uptime is possible and your team can chain objectives.

items.name: Socks
items.buyAdvice: Buy before deeper floors, blackout pressure, or long-route pacing.

items.name: Waffle
items.effect: Grants 30% speed and full stamina regen.
items.source: Infected Weffle conditions or Weffle teammate utility
items.buyAdvice: Save for chase breaks, elevator sprints, or low-stamina recoveries.

items.name: Medkit
items.buyAdvice: Reserve for severely injured players or support anchors that must stay alive.

items.name: Bandaid
items.buyAdvice: Cheap stabilization for teammates entering danger on the next floor.

items.name: USB
items.buyAdvice: Buy when timer pressure is high or exposed computers are too risky to hold.

items.name: Wrench
items.buyAdvice: Use to bypass dangerous machine positions or close a floor quickly.

items.name: Sunny's Shop
items.effect: Shows three random items between even-numbered floors.
items.buyAdvice: Choose based on immediate team risk: healing, objective speed, or mobility.

items.name: Chekhov's Shop
items.effect: Sells agents, cosmetics, and run-saving utility items.
items.buyAdvice: Prioritize survival and objective consistency before cosmetic or low-impact buys.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Toons Universe Research, Trinkets, and Emblems
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Progression is driven by Malachor income, research completion, and level-100 emblem unlocks. Plan these tracks around the role you actually play.

tracks.requirement: Clear floors, fill machines, collect research capsules, and complete milestones.
tracks.reward: Main currency for agent and skin purchases.
tracks.knownValues: Typical values include floor clears, machine completion, and capsule pickups with card-based modifiers.
tracks.bestUse: Unlock role-critical agents first, then pivot to long-term upgrades.

tracks.reward: Direct Malachor and infected research progression.
tracks.knownValues: Capsule value scales better when your team can hold stable routing.
tracks.bestUse: Take safe capsules without sacrificing machine tempo or survival.

tracks.reward: Research trinket unlocks tied to completion milestones.
tracks.knownValues: Long-grind objective; progress is strongest in consistent survival teams.
tracks.bestUse: Treat as secondary grind after your clear rate is reliable.

tracks.requirement: Level agents through repeated floor runs.
tracks.reward: Milestone Malachor plus level-100 emblem unlocks.
tracks.knownValues: Milestones scale across levels and culminate in each agent emblem.
tracks.bestUse: Focus one agent to 100 when targeting a specific build effect.

tracks.knownValues: Only two emblems can be equipped at once.
tracks.bestUse: Pair one survivability emblem with one objective-speed emblem.

tracks.requirement: Reach level 100 on Nimbus or Weffle.
tracks.reward: Lower stamina consumption or objective-generated Waffle utility.
tracks.knownValues: Excellent for runner and support-heavy teams.
tracks.bestUse: Prioritize when your squads lose pace during chase windows.

tracks.requirement: Reach level 100 on Teipo or Squiddle.
tracks.reward: Team stamina boosts and extraction head starts.
tracks.knownValues: Both emblems directly improve objective pacing.
tracks.bestUse: Strong in machine-heavy runs where clear speed decides survival.

tracks.requirement: Reach level 100 on Selena or Patcher.
tracks.reward: Objective speed buffs and periodic healing support.
tracks.knownValues: Useful for teams that absorb repeated chip damage.
tracks.bestUse: Run these when sustain is your bottleneck past mid floors.

tracks.track: Koi, Guido and Rossum, Fizzle Emblems
tracks.reward: Cooldown relief, computer highlights, and early-floor sanity protection.
tracks.knownValues: Information and pacing emblems for coordinated routing.
tracks.bestUse: Best in squads that already communicate callouts consistently.

tracks.reward: Boosted item impact, early-floor shielding, and objective-triggered healing.
tracks.knownValues: High value for long sessions with stacked objective completions.
tracks.bestUse: Use when pushing deeper floors where attrition usually ends runs.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Track alpha status, trailer milestones, upcoming mechanics, and release signals from official channels in one timeline.

entries.playerImpact: Treat the Roblox page as the live source for build status, servers, and store context.

entries.currentInfo: The game is set in INNOVA, focused on resource routing, hazards, and agent team utility.
entries.playerImpact: Confirms that survival routing and machine tempo remain core strategy topics.

entries.currentInfo: Character additions were logged in April 2026 while the page remains alpha-labeled.
entries.playerImpact: Use alpha label for live status and additions for early roadmap tracking.

entries.currentInfo: Recent additions shape healing, shielding, and scouting role coverage.
entries.playerImpact: Role balance changes influence team composition and unlock priority.
entries.refreshTrigger: Ability kits, costs, or unlock rule changes

entries.currentInfo: Multiple character model updates are visible in ongoing preview material.
entries.playerImpact: Preview waves indicate likely future unlocks and role rebalancing.

entries.currentInfo: New infected variants are teased in preview updates.
entries.playerImpact: Enemy additions can force major route, sanity, and item-priority changes.
entries.refreshTrigger: Infected behavior enters the live build

entries.currentInfo: Laboratory floor teaser variants are already circulating.
entries.playerImpact: Map changes alter machine paths, capsule safety, and elevator timing.

entries.currentInfo: Previewed systems include item transfer, shop voting, and elevator card selection.
entries.playerImpact: These systems will change economy calls and team coordination rules.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use these official destinations for gameplay access, social announcements, trailers, and reference navigation.

links.bestFor: Playing and checking current public build status

faq.title: Toons Universe
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for codes, progression, and run strategy

questions.question: How often do Toons Universe codes change?
questions.answer: Code drops are irregular, so checking official channels and update trackers weekly is the most reliable routine.

questions.question: Which starter is better for solo runs?
questions.answer: Nimbus is usually the most forgiving solo starter because stamina recovery helps recover from chase mistakes.

questions.question: What should we do when panic mode starts?
questions.answer: Stop side looting, call a safe return route, and commit the whole team to elevator extraction immediately.

questions.question: Is Malachor better spent on cosmetics or agents first?
questions.answer: Early progression is generally smoother when currency is focused on role-critical agent unlocks first.

questions.question: How do we improve public lobby clear rates?
questions.answer: Keep machine assignments simple, avoid over-splitting, and rotate support abilities before stamina collapses.

questions.question: Where can I find official updates and trailer posts?
questions.answer: Use the Roblox experience page, official Discord, and the Toon Shenanigans YouTube channel for update tracking.

cta.title: Ready for Toons Universe Runs?
cta.description: Join the Discord community for update alerts, then queue Roblox runs with a clearer floor plan.

footer.title: Toons Universe Wiki
footer.description: A focused resource hub for codes, agents, infected guides, floor systems, and progression planning.

codes.title: Toons Universe Codes
codes.description: Redeem active codes for gems, stat chips, and rerolls so you can speed up early progression and team upgrades.
codes.metaTitle: Toons Universe Codes - Free Gems, Chips, and Rerolls
codes.metaDescription: Find active Toons Universe codes for gems, stat chips, and rerolls. Use the latest redemption list to boost your progress quickly.
codes.whatIsDescription: Codes are limited-time rewards released by the developers. They usually grant gems, stat chips, and rerolls that help you build stronger runs without extra grinding.

whySteps.step1Description: Get a clean list of active rewards so you can redeem everything in one pass.
whySteps.step2Description: Check update timing to avoid missing short code drops tied to events or milestones.
whySteps.step3Description: Use bonus gems and chips on the upgrades that matter most for your next runs.

characters.title: Toons Universe Characters
characters.description: Compare agents, unlock paths, and role strengths so you can pick the best roster for solo and team runs.
characters.metaTitle: Toons Universe Characters - Agent Roles and Unlock Guide
characters.metaDescription: Explore Toons Universe character guides with role breakdowns, unlock methods, and team fit recommendations for every run style.
characters.whatIsDescription: Characters are playable agents with different utility profiles, scaling patterns, and synergy options. Choosing the right one changes your pacing, safety, and objective control.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand which agents are easiest to pilot during early account progression.
whySteps.step2Description: Match support, control, and objective roles for stable co-op clears.
whySteps.step3Description: Spend currencies on agents that scale well with your preferred strategy.

infected.title: Toons Universe Infected
infected.description: Learn enemy behavior, pressure windows, and counterplay routes for infected threats across different floors.
infected.metaTitle: Toons Universe Infected - Enemy Patterns and Counterplay
infected.metaDescription: Study Toons Universe infected enemies with behavior notes, danger triggers, and practical counterplay for safer floor progression.
infected.whatIsDescription: Infected are hostile threats with unique movement, detection, and punish patterns. Each variant demands different positioning, timing, and team communication.

whySteps.step1Description: Recognize high-risk patterns early and route around danger before it escalates.
whySteps.step2Description: Keep machines and floor objectives active while managing enemy pressure.
whySteps.step3Description: Use clear role assignments when kiting, peeling, or resetting threat lines.

guide.title: Toons Universe Guide
guide.description: Step-by-step progression, survival loops, and floor strategy notes for beginners and experienced players.
guide.metaTitle: Toons Universe Guide - Beginner and Advanced Progression
guide.metaDescription: Read Toons Universe guide content covering beginner setup, survival tactics, floor routing, and practical progression decisions.
guide.whatIsDescription: The guide category collects broad strategy content, from first-session fundamentals to efficient routes for long runs and consistent resource gains.

whySteps.step1Description: Follow structured early-game priorities instead of learning through costly trial and error.
whySteps.step2Description: Apply repeatable routing and objective habits that stay useful on higher floors.
whySteps.step3Description: Revisit route logic and strategy baselines as mechanics and balance shift.

emblems.title: Toons Universe Emblems
emblems.description: Break down emblem effects, stacking behavior, and loadout choices to maximize each run.
emblems.metaTitle: Toons Universe Emblems - Effects, Stacking, and Loadouts
emblems.metaDescription: Review Toons Universe emblem guides with effect details, stacking rules, and loadout planning for offense, utility, and survival.
emblems.whatIsDescription: Emblems are build modifiers that shape your run through passive boosts and utility effects. They can shift your damage curve, consistency, and resource efficiency.

whySteps.step1Description: Separate high-impact effects from options that only look strong on paper.
whySteps.step2Description: Learn which mixes conflict and which stacks create reliable synergy.
whySteps.step3Description: Swap emblem priorities for farming runs, boss pressure, or safe floor climbing.

stats.title: Toons Universe Stats
stats.description: Understand stamina, sanity, checks, and scaling breakpoints so your upgrades produce consistent returns.
stats.metaTitle: Toons Universe Stats - Scaling, Checks, and Upgrade Priorities
stats.metaDescription: Learn Toons Universe stat systems including stamina, sanity, machine checks, and upgrade breakpoints for efficient progression.
stats.whatIsDescription: Stats define your baseline performance in movement, survival, machine handling, and consistency under pressure. Smart allocation improves both speed and safety.

whySteps.step1Description: Invest where thresholds unlock noticeably smoother clears and fewer failed checks.
whySteps.step2Description: Avoid overspending on one axis while neglecting stamina, sanity, or utility needs.
whySteps.step3Description: Align stat choices with your agent kit and your team responsibility in runs.

cosmetics.title: Toons Universe Cosmetics
cosmetics.description: Track skins, visual variants, and style collections across events, passes, and themed releases.
cosmetics.metaTitle: Toons Universe Cosmetics - Skins and Visual Collections
cosmetics.metaDescription: Browse Toons Universe cosmetics coverage for skins, themed variants, event visuals, and collection-focused release tracking.
cosmetics.whatIsDescription: Cosmetics are visual customizations such as skins and style variants that let you personalize your profile and roster identity without changing core mechanics.

whySteps.step1Description: Monitor event windows and pass rotations to collect styles before they cycle out.
whySteps.step2Description: Prioritize purchases that fit your favorite agents and long-term collection goals.
whySteps.step3Description: Review release themes so you can decide which bundles are worth saving for.
